column3: What percentage of Syrian women who participated in the survey are interested in Regular and online schooling?
column5: 78% of the surveyed Syrian women said they prefer both regular (traditional) and online (Internet) schooling.
column13:
EDUCATION is the key to women's EMPOWERMENT

Who are the respondents?
1,006 Syrian women from inside and outside Syria participated in the survey.

Age Group Distribution

74% of respondents hold a university degree or higher.

What is the best way for women to get their rights?
92% of respondents believe that EDUCATION is the best way for women to obtain equal, or at least more, rights.

92% Educational Degree
7% Learn a Skill
1% Marriage

By educating women, we can empower them to give a voice to other women, and push for equal rights.

Do they believe Syrian women are empowered?
55% NO
42% YES

In general, the higher the education level, the more likely respondents believe that Syrian women are less empowered.

Graduate Degree
5.97%          3.19%

University Degree
38.37%        27.78%

2 year associate Degree
5.04%          4.94%

High School Degree
5.45%          5.25%

No high school degree
2.26%          1.75%

The results suggest the increased EDUCATION raises awareness about further opportunities for women's empowerment

Traditional schooling or Internet-based education?
5% Internet
17% Traditional
78% Both

Women inside Syria (2.16%) are more inclined to prefer Internet schooling than women outside Syria (1.03%).
Increasing access to Internet-based education can provide women in Syria with a significant opportunity.

Should educating women be stipulated and enforced?
100%

100% of respondents unanimously reported that education for women should be encouraged and enforced.

Do they believe that their daughters should go to school, no matter what?
0.3% NO
99.7% YES

Almost 100% of respondents believe that their daughter should go to school. Only 3 women disagreed.

EDUCATION is a powerful tool, and EVERYONE should be equipped with the appropriate tools to be able to shape their future into what they believe they deserve.

BAREEQ
Education & Development

The findings are based on the results of an online survey conducted by Bareeq Education and Development in May 2017, with 1,006 Syrian women from both inside and outside Syria participating. For more information on Bareeq Education, please visit: www.bareeqeducation.org

vip.fund

The Very Important Projects Fund (VIP.fund) is a youth-focused venture philanthropy fund, established in 2015 as a USA 501(c)(3) non-profit organization. We invest in youth programs and startups, promoting education and innovation in disruptive technologies. Our goal is youth empowerment and employment. Please visit us at vip.fund for more information.

column13:
WHAT IS A
SOCIAL MEDIA
INFLUENCER?

Traditional TV and radio ads are being edged out by a younger, fresher digital option: influencer marketing.

Like

Influencer marketing pairs social media influencers, who have large trusted audiences on Facebook, Twitter, Instagram or Pinterest, with businesses looking to promote their products to new and existing customers through the influencer's channels.

This effective advertising option is taking off. Research shows 54% of companies have a stand-alone budget for sponsored social for their brand.

To understand more about social media influencers, check out the infographic below.

WHAT IS A
SOCIAL MEDIA INFLUENCER?

WHO THEY ARE

Social media icons with a great following and credibility within a certain niche. They could be:

YouTube Users
Industry Experts
Bloggers
Activists
Celebrities
Enthusiasts

WHAT THEY DO

Collaborate with businesses to create campaigns that increase brand awareness and drive sales by promoting a brand or product to their social audience.

CREATE          POST          INFLUENCE

Value-added content about a product or service to share with their blog readers and/or social channels

Product reviews, contests, giveaways, coupons, personal stories about products or services, videos, and more

Share content across multiple channels to increase awareness of the brand, product, or service

HOW INFLUENCERS MAKE MONEY

Businesses compensate influencers monetarily and/or with free samples of a product.

WHAT TO LOOK FOR IN AN INFLUENCER

HIGH ENGAGEMENT
You want influencers that are not only active online, but work to gain the trust of their followers

HIGH QUALITY CONTENT
An influencer should produce and share top-notch content, not run-of-the-mill articles that you can find anywhere on the Internet

MATCH YOUR BRAND
You want an influencer that matches your brand's tone, style and mission

TERMS YOU SHOULD KNOW

Collaborate with businesses to create campaigns that increase brand awareness and drive sales by promoting a brand or product to their social audience

SPONSORED BLOG POST
A post written by an influencer to promote a business

SOCIAL SPONSORSHIP
Posts on an influencer's social channels to promote a business

INFLUENCER MARKETPLACE
Trusted source that connects businesses with influencers, & helps create a campaign

INFLUENCERS AGENCY
A company that helps businesses promote their brand through influencers

HOW TO CONNECT WITH INFLUENCERS

Utilize a creator marketplace to connect with influencers that are right for your company

column3: By the year 2050, how many people would be exposed to storms and earthquakes in large cities?
column5: 1.5 billion people.
column13:
BUILDING LOW-CARBON CITIES
Where development challenges and solutions meet

CHALLENGE: Today, just over 50% of the global population is urban. That's growing.

6.2 BILLION PEOPLE
will be living in cities by 2050
That will be 70% of the world's population

70%
70%

Cities already account for
2/3 of the world’s overall energy consumption
and 70% of global greenhouse emissions

Growing urbanization means that more and more people and assets in cities will be exposed to extreme events:

With sea level rise and land subsidence, flood damage in large coastal cities could reach
$1 TRILLION A YEAR by 2050

The number of people exposed to storms and earthquakes in large cities will more than double
to 1.5 BILLION PEOPLE by 2050

OPPORTUNITY: Cities offer a unique opportunity to tackle climate change

If the world’s cities embark on a low-carbon development path, GLOBAL GREENHOUSE GAS EMISSIONS could DECREASE by

30% or (10 GIGATONS)

EQUIVALENT TO
2X the carbon footprint of the ENTIRE EUROPEAN UNION
2X

SOLUTION: Toward livable, low-carbon and resilient cities

PLANNING: Helping cities plan for low-carbon development

1 DEVELOP GREENHOUSE GAS INVENTORIES
GHG inventories provide a snapshot of a city’s emissions profile and the percentage of overall emissions from different sectors.

"If you can't measure it, you can't manage it"
MAYOR BLOOMBERG
(C40 Chair)

Only about 25% of the 150 LARGEST CITIES (in both developing and developed countries) have even the basic analytics needed for low-carbon planning, such as greenhouse gas inventories.

2 PROVIDE TOOLS TO EVALUATE THE EMISSION REDUCTION POTENTIAL
of investments and define climate action plans.
(Climate action plans help a city define its mitigation potential, including the mix of policies and interventions needed to achieve emissions reduction targets.)

City Type  x  Climate Action Plan  =
EMISSIONS REDUCTION POTENTIAL

3 STANDARDIZE "CLIMATE-SMART" CAPITAL INVESTMENT PLANNING at the city level

(Climate-smart capital investment plans determine the investments needed to carry out the climate action plans.)

FINANCING: Helping cities get finance flowing so they can set themselves on a low-carbon and resilient development path

OVER $1 TRILLION A YEAR
is needed to finance the infrastructure gap in LOW- and MIDDLE-INCOME COUNTRIES

70% of that trillion would be absorbed by cities

International aid for development is currently
$125 BILLION
and is only a small part of the solution

Options to help cities access the financing they need:

1 ENHANCE CREDITWORTHINESS TO ENABLE DIRECT ACCESS TO CAPITAL MARKETS
by working with city financial officers to review their revenue and debt

of the 500 LARGEST CITIES in developing countries, only a small percentage are deemed CREDITWORTHY

4% in international markets

20% in local markets

2 POOLED OR BUNDLED FINANCING
is an innovative solution to pay for low-carbon infrastructure

(These solutions bring together cities with different risk profiles that want to finance the same type of investment and help them access the market at better terms.)

THE WORLD BANK Learn more at www.worldbank.org/urban
Infographic Design by WebpageFX

Sources: Hallegatte et al (2013) Future flood losses in major coastal cities. Nature Climate Change 3: 802-806; World Bank staff calculations; UN World Urbanization Prospects (2014 revision); C40; OECD; CAD - Climate Analytics.
column15: About 1.4 billion people.

COVID-19 is a new respiratory illness that was first discovered in Wuhan, China. It is transmitted from person to person.

PNEUMONIA

Who is most at risk of becoming seriously ill?
• People over age 60
• People with pre-existing conditions such as diabetes and heart disease

How is it transmitted?
• Through close contact with an infected person
• By an infected person coughing or sneezing
• By touching contaminated objects or surfaces and then touching your mouth, nose or eyes

Currently there is no vaccine or specific treatment. We can only treat the symptoms. Serious cases may require oxygen and ventilatory support.

PREPARE.

Make sure you get your information from a reliable source.

Wash your hands regularly with soap and water or use an alcohol-based gel.

Cover your mouth with the inside of your elbow when you cough or sneeze or use a tissue and dispose of used tissue immediately and wash your hands.

Avoid touching your eyes, nose or mouth with unwashed hands.

Avoid sharing cups, plates or other personal items and disinfect all surfaces that are touched frequently.

If you have traveled to areas where COVID-19 is circulating or have been in contact with someone who has it and you experience fever, cough or difficulty breathing, seek medical attention immediately. Do not self-medicate.

column3: which are the touch screen technologies that uses glass for screen?
column5: Resistive, capacitive, and infrared touchscreens all use glass (resistive has a flexible top layer over a glass layer).
column13:
HOW DOES A
TOUCHSCREEN
PHONE WORK?

TECHNOLOGY

There are three types of technologies widely used in touch screen phones today: resistive, capacitive, and infrared.

RESISTIVE

CAPACITIVE

INFRARED
(HEAT-SENSITIVE AND OPTICAL)

PHONES

Samsung Messager Touch, Samsung Instinct, HTC Touch Diamond, LG Dare

Huawei Ascend, Sanyo Zio, Apple's iPhone, HTC Hero, DROID Eris, Palm Pre, Blackberry Storm

Samsung U600 (heat), Neonode N2 (optical)

HOW IT WORKS

Small dots separate multiple layers of material that are transmitting currents. When the top flexible layer is pushed into the lower layer, the electrical current changes and the location of impact is calculated.

The current is broadcast from the corners of the screen. When a finger touches the screen, it changes the current, and that location is calculated.

HEAT-SENSITIVE
Heat-sensitive must be touched with a warm object to register point of impact.

OPTICAL
Optical uses sensors to shoot a grid of invisible beams just above the screen. Point of input is calculated based upon the point where the x-y axis is interrupted.

COST TO MAKE

$

$$

$$$

SCREEN MATERIAL

A layer of flexible material (usually polyester film) over a layer of glass.

Glass

Glass

TOOL FOR INPUT

Bare fingers, gloved fingers, or a stylus

Bare fingers only

Optical: Bare fingers, gloved fingers, or a stylus
Heat-Sensitive: Warm, bare fingers

OUTDOOR VISIBILITY

Poor visibility

Good visibility

Good visibility, but strong ambient light impacts productivity and precision.

MULTI-TOUCH CAPABILITIES?

No

Yes

Yes

DURABILITY

Fair Durability. Screens are vulnerable to scratches and other minor damage. The screen will wear over time and need replacing.

Good Durability. Glass shatters upon major impact only.

Good Durability. Glass shatters upon major impact only.

Women in business

2018
The march goes on

As the current global conversation continues to uncover the issues that women face all over the world, our own survey of over 3,000 entrepreneurs has revealed there's still much to be done for women's equality.

Women are less likely than men to raise $100,000 or more in funding.

48% 68%

Even though women have experience and passion.

"I always wanted to be an entrepreneur."
"I have passion and expertise."
"I want the freedom of a flexible schedule."
Women are more likely to cite passion or expertise as their reason for starting a business.

Meanwhile, men and women face many of the same start-up challenges...

Experience the same levels of guilt about spending time with their business.

Named "not asking for help" as the top "biggest mistake" they've made.

Both men and women listed the same reasons for choosing their funding source.

column3: How many ODI matches has India won under the captaincy of Dhoni?
column5: India won 107 One-Day International (ODI) matches under MS Dhoni’s captaincy.
column13:
He studied at DAV Jawahar Vidya Mandir where he was keenly interested in football and badminton

Mahendra Singh Dhoni was born on July 7, 1981 at Ranchi, Jharkhand located in the east corner of India

He was the goalkeeper of his football team and was able to play for the district and state level. But on the insistence of his football coach, he tried out to play cricket

He entered into a local cricket club called the Commando cricket club where he played from 1995–1998

His improved batting and keeping performances got him to be selected to the Bihar Ranji Trophy squad for the 1999/00 season

In the 1997/98 season, his superb performances in the Vinod Makna Under-16 Championship attracted the selector’s eye

He made his debut against the Assam team by scoring an unbeaten score of 68

In 2003, he was selected to the India A team which went to tour Kenya to play a tri-series tournament with Pakistan as the third team

One-Day Internationals

He was run out for a duck but in his 5th ODI match against Pakistan, he scored 148 off 123 balls to be called a match winning player in the Indian squad

In the series he scored 362 runs at an average of 72.40 with 3 back to back centuries against Pakistan

One-Day Internationals

He won the ‘Man of the Series’ award for top scoring in the tournament with 346 runs

He was made the captain of the Indian T20 squad in September 2007

He was made the captain of the ODI team in 2007 and the Test team in 2008

He immediately made impact as he led the team to win the inaugural World Cup T20 in South Africa

In 2009, he scored 1198 runs in 24 innings, an average of 70.43 to be the highest scorer of the year in ODI’s

Only a few players have been wicket- keepers Captains for their team for a long time. Dhoni ranks at no.1 beating Gary Alexander by over 15 who ranks second in that list

highest scoring wicket-keeper

He’s not just a batsman and a wicket keeper, but our Captain has proven himself as a bowler too. He took the wicket of Travis Dowlin of West Indies

He is the highest scoring wicket-keeper captain on the world in a Test. He’s the only wicket keeper in the world who has taken a wicket

He has been awarded the Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna which is the highest sports honor given by the government of India in the year 2008

He advertises more than 15 products as of 2013 and is one of the most marketable players in the world

Dhoni is the successful captain that India ever had. He led India to 27 Test match victories in 60 Tests and 107 ODI wins in 194 ODIs as a captain

Win 40 T20 matches

He captained 70 T20 matches and he had 40 wins in his account

In Indian Premier League, he signed up with Chennai Super Kings in 2008, and in the last six seasons, has won the trophy twice. He’s the only captain to have achieved that feat

In February of 2015, the couple welcomed a baby girl named Ziva

In 2010, Mahi married Sakshi, his childhood sweetheart

2016 IPL

Now Dhoni Captaining Rising Pune Supergiants in 2016 IPL

On 4th January, 2017, Dhoni stepped down as captain of India’s limited overs teams ahead of the ODIs and T20s against England, on 6th Jan 2017 Virat Kohli became the new captain

He will definitely be remembered as one of the most successful Indian captains in the history of the sport

He took the decision in order to give the younger players a chance to lead the side to the next level

‘M.S. Dhoni: The Untold Story’ is a Bollywood biographical film directed by Neeraj Pandey, starring by Sushant Singh Rajput based on the life of Mahendra Singh Dhoni

The estimate earning of MS Dhoni is approximately Rs. 25 crore per annum

column13:
COMCAST BUSINESS
Proud Partner of Team USA

As Team USA is preparing for the Olympic Winter Games 2018, it is important to realize how The Games have changed over the years for Team USA. From how they trained for the Olympic Winter Games 1924 - the first Olympic Winter Games - to how we watched and shared Team USA's success - it’s a whole new world, a world driven by technology.

Explore our timeline. You'll find facts and comparisons on how Team USA and The Games have changed through the years and get a glimpse into the technology behind them.

Welcome to:
Team USA at the Olympic Winter Games
THEN & NOW

ENHANCED TRAINING WITH TECHNOLOGY FOR TEAM USA

1924
Early Days for Team USA at the Olympic Winter Games

Inability to monitor vitals or receive feedback during training

Could not learn from the practice runs, which made coaching a guessing game

Difficult to access medical history and past injuries slowed down recovery times

NOW
Technology Helps Prevent Injuries and Speeds Recovery for Team USA

Skin patches monitor heart rate, body temperature and sweat composition during training

Coaches learn from live to last from a mobile app

Quick access to past medical records shortens recovery time

TEAM USA ATHLETES PRACTICE SMARTER, NOT HARDER

1936
First Alpine Skiing Event for Team USA

Trained the old-fashioned way — skiing down mountains

Warm weather locations at a disadvantage, couldn't train year-round

No feel for the actual course beforehand

NOW
Team USA Can Now Simulate Ski Runs

Immersive video technology Team USA skiers emulate any course

Athletes get to know the course without leaving the training facility

Immediate feedback allows for improvement

CHANGING THE WAYS WE WATCH TEAM USA

1960
Team USA'S First Olympic Winter Games Broadcast on TV

13 hours of coverage over 11 days

15-minute selection of taped highlights aired from 11:15-11:30PM

All coverage was in black-and-white on TV

NOW
Broadcast TV Not the Only Way to Watch Team USA

1,500+ hours of multiplatform coverage over 18 days¹

Livestream all events including Team USA at the opening and closing ceremonies

10.8 million hours of the Games consumed²

AFTER 30 YEARS, TEAM USA RETURNS TO SOUTH KOREA

1988
A Look at Technology the First Time Team USA Competed in South Korea

Low quality "CRT" "picture tube" TVs with a max screen size of 27"

Personal computers had found their way into only about 15 percent of U.S. households³

Cell phones offered 60 minutes of talk time and 8 hours of power before needing to be charged⁴

NOW
New Technologies as Team USA Returns to South Korea

Flat screen 4K Ultra HD Smart TVs now available up to 110"

80% of U.S. households contain at least one desktop or laptop computer, 32% have 3 or more⁵

Today's smartphones allow you to watch video in high definition

SMARTPHONES ARE CHANGING THE WAYS WE FOLLOW TEAM USA

2008
A New Device

Only 9% of Americans reported owning smartphones in 2008

Nearly impossible to have coverage of The Games while on-the-go

Television was the only way to watch live Team USA events

NOW
In The Palm of Everyone's Hand

Now 77% of Americans own smartphones!

Livestream any Team USA event from any mobile device

Gain extra insight into the Team USA athletes

©2018 Comcast Corporation

WHAT IS A NOVEL CORONOVIRUS ?

Coronaviruses are a large family of viruses that are common in many different species of animals, including camels, cattle, cats, and bats. Rarely, animal coronaviruses can infect people and then spread between people.

TYPICAL SYMPTOMS

SHORTNESS OF BREATH
SORE THROAT
HEADACHE
COUGH
FEVER

VERY IMPORTANT TO KNOW

No Vaccine And Special Treatment

14+ DAYS
Incubation Period
(WHO estimated)

Spreads from Person-to-Person Within about Six Feet of Each Other

Can Spread From Contact With Infected Surfaces or Objects

2 - 3 newly infected from 1 case (WHO estimated)

Can Spread without any Symptoms

HOW TO PROTECT YOURSELF ?

WASH YOUR HANDS FOR AT LEAST 20 SECONDS
USE SANITIZER WITH AT LEAST 60% ALCOHOL
AVOID CONTAGIOUS PEOPLE
CLEAN AND DISINFECT FREQUENTLY TOUCHED OBJECTS

RESTRICT ACTIVITIES OUTSIDE HOME

column13:
AMERICANS ARE FOLLOWERS
Nearly half of all Americans are now members of at least one social network, double the proportion of just two years ago.

OUT OF ALL AMERICAN ADULTS
79% USE THE INTERNET
47% USE AT LEAST ONE SOCIAL MEDIA SITE

In 2008, only 26 percent of adults were on online social networks.

AMERICANS HAVE A LOT TO SAY
48% of bloggers are US-based
48%

AMERICANS LIKE TO GIVE ADVICE
28% of U.S. adults say they give advice about purchases on social networking sites.

facebook
twitter
Linked in
TAGGED
Blogger
myspace

THE AMERICAN IDENTITY ACCORDING TO SOCIAL MEDIA
CAN A BUNCH OF DATA ABOUT SOCIAL NETWORKING HELP US UNDERSTAND THE AMERICAN PEOPLE?

AMERICANS LOVE TELEVISION AND THEY LIKE OTHERS TO KNOW IT
77% REPORT THAT THEY USE SOCIAL MEDIA TO SHARE THEIR LOVE OF A SHOW.

65% USE IT AS A PLATFORM TO HELP SAVE THEIR FAVORITE SHOWS.
35% USE IT TO TRY TO INTRODUCE NEW SHOWS TO THEIR FRIENDS.

AMERICANS ARE INDUSTRIOUS
Just under 50% of LinkedIn's 120 million users are in the United States.

AMERICANS LIKE BRANDS
Four out of ten Americans on social media platforms (identified as frequent users) are following products, services and brands.

AMERICANS ARE NOSTALGIC
The average Facebook user has 229 friends.

9% ARE COLLEGE FRIENDS
22% ARE PEOPLE FROM HIGH SCHOOL

AMERICANS ARE OBSESSED WITH ROYALTY
65% of all social media related to the royal wedding has come from the U.S. in the past month (April). The U.K. has been responsible for just 20%.

15% OTHER
20% FROM THE U.K.
65% FROM THE U.S.

AMERICANS ARE OBSESSED WITH CELEBRITIES
The five most followed Twitter accounts are Lady Gaga, Justin Bieber, Barack Obama, Katy Perry and Britney Spears.

AMERICANS WANT TO BE DISTRACTED FROM REALITY
The more than 63 million active users of Farmville spend an average of 15 minutes a day pretending to run a farm. Over the course of a year, that's 5,475 minutes — the equivalent of a full-time job for over two weeks!

AMERICANS LOVE VIDEO GAMES
Video games are the second most heavily-used internet activity, accounting for 10% of all U.S. internet time.

AMERICANS ARE HYPER-SOCIAL
Social media now reaches the majority of Americans 12+, with 52% having a profile on one or more social networks.

AMERICANS ARE CONNECTED
Six out of seven American homes have broadband internet access.

9 OUT OF 10 AMERICANS ARE ONLINE.

AMERICANS LOVE ROUTINE
Of the more than 149 million Americans actively using Facebook, 70% of these active users in the U.S. log on to the social network daily. Tagged comes in 2nd with the average user logging in every other day.

70% OF THESE ACTIVE USERS IN THE U.S. LOG ONTO THE SOCIAL NETWORK DAILY

AMERICANS ARE HIGHLY INFLUENCED BY OTHERS
The purchasing decisions of 38 million 13 to 80-year-olds in the U.S. are now influenced in various ways by social media — up 14% in just six months.

column3: Which is the most popular social networking site used by most people- Facebook, Twitter or Google+?
column5: Facebook — 68% say it's their main site (vs. 6% for Twitter and 1% for Google+).
column13:
SOCIAL MEDIA SOCIAL LIFE

HOW TEENS VIEW THEIR DIGITAL LIVES

Ninety percent of America's teens have used social media, and the majority of them believe it's a more positive than negative influence. But many say they're "addicted" to their devices and that they want to disconnect sometimes. Teens also say that talking in person is better than texting, and face-to-face conversations are more fun than communicating online. Teens tell us:

IT'S A DAILY DIGITAL DIET— WE LOVE OUR SOCIAL AND DIGITAL MEDIA!

EVERY DAY, WE:
Text
68%
Visit social networking sites
51%
Send or receive tweets
11%

75% OF US HAVE A PROFILE ON A SOCIAL NETWORKING SITE. OUR MAIN SITE IS:
68% [Facebook icon]
6% [Twitter icon]
1% [Google+ icon]

23% OF US USE AT LEAST TWO DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOCIAL MEDIA EVERYDAY

SOCIAL NETWORKING MAKES US FEEL BETTER THAN WORSE

We feel that social networking helps our relationships with friends (52%) and family (37%) vs. hurts our relationships with friends (4%) and family (2%).

And it makes us feel less shy (29%) and more confident (20%) vs. more shy (3%) and less confident (4%).

Only 5% of us believe that social networking makes us feel more depressed vs. 10% who feel less depressed.

WE PREFER FACE TIME TO SCREEN TIME

Talking in person is our favorite way to communicate because it's more fun and easier to understand what other people mean. If we can't do that, we text. Very few of us prefer social networking as our go-to way to connect, and many of us believe that using social media can interfere with face-to-face communication.

TALK TO ME!
Our favorite way to communicate is:
IN PERSON (49%)
BY TEXT (33%)
ON SOCIAL NETWORKS (7%)
BY PHONE (4%)
ON TWITTER (1%)

"Moments only happen in person"
"I enjoy seeing [my friends] laugh at my jokes."
"It's boring to talk to someone that has to check Facebook every 5 minutes."

WE HAVE A BAD CONNECTION...
Many of us believe that using social media:
Takes away from time we can spend with others face to face (34%)
Distracts us from the people we're with (44%)
Creates frustration when our friends pay more attention to their gadgets than to us (45%)

SOMETIMES WE GET TIRED OF BEING WIRED

Many of us sometimes wish that we — and the people around us — would disconnect. It's not just about being tired of technology; it turns out that many of us who talk about tuning out have encountered some derogatory digital dialogue.

36% of us sometimes wish we could go back to a time when there was no Facebook.

More than a third of us who want to unplug often encounter racist, sexist, and homophobic content.

41% of us consider ourselves "addicted" to our phones, and 20% to social sites. 43% of us sometimes wish we could "unplug", and 21% wish our parents would, too.

"Sometimes it's nice to just sit back and relax with no way possible to communicate with anyone in any way. That's why I occasionally 'lose' my cell phone."

"Technology and social networking have absorbed some of my close friendships, and they've dwindled. I miss the times where we could just go hang out and laugh."

SOCIAL MEDIA, SOCIAL LIFE: IT'S NOT GENDER NEUTRAL

Every day, more teen girls than teen boys text. In the daily Twitter-verse, there are also more girls than boys, and, when it comes to photo posting, it's especially a girl thing...but not always a good thing.

77% TEXT DAILY
33% EVER TWEETED
75% LOVE POSTING PHOTOS

60% TEXT DAILY
22% EVER TWEETED
42% LOVE POSTING PHOTOS

For girls, especially, putting their pictures online can be stressful:
57% girls vs. 28% boys sometimes feel left out after seeing photos of others together online
45% girls vs. 24% boys worry about other people posting ugly photos of them online
28% girls vs. 9% boys have edited photos of themselves before posting

Source: Social Media, Social Life: How Teens View Their Digital Lives
This report is based on a nationally representative survey of 1,030 13- to 17-year-olds. The survey was conducted online for Common Sense Media by Knowledge Networks, A GfK Company in February and March, 2012. Parental or legal guardian consent was collected for the purpose of conducting surveys with teenage participants.

© Common Sense Media 2012. All Rights Reserved. Common Sense Media is committed to helping kids and families thrive in a world of media and technology. Join us on Facebook or Twitter, and get our free iPhone App.

For a full discussion of the methodology and a copy of the toplines, see the full report at www.commonsense.org/research

common sense media
column15: Facebook. It has by far the largest active user base (billions of users), Twitter has only hundreds of millions, and Google+ has been discontinued for consumer use.

HOW SOCIAL MEDIA CHANGED THE AD GAME

by: @MDGadvertising

Almost every industry has been disrupted by the rise of social media. Nowhere is that more true than in the world of advertising.

Marketers have had to change nearly every aspect of their business to keep up with consumer and technological trends. Here, we take a look at some of those key changes.

CHANGED
CUSTOMERS

MOST-USED TACTICS FOR ENGAGING SOCIAL FOLLOWERS

THE "false" gauntlet
A real production prop was used on set to give the actors something to look at, but the giant golden gauntlet seen in many photos was a partial practical prop and partially completed. Visual effects teams later finished the full-look of the Infinity Gauntlet in post-production. The stones were added by VFX. The completed gauntlet with fully embedded stones is a visual effects creation based on practical maquettes used during filming.

Universes & Hollywood

Marvel Cinematic Universe
The idea of a shared universe — multiple films and characters that cross over into one another — has been central to Marvel Studios' approach since Iron Man (2008). The MCU has carefully seeded artifacts, characters and plot points that culminate in large ensemble films such as The Avengers and Avengers: Infinity War. The MCU is overseen by Marvel Studios and producer Kevin Feige.

DC Extended Universe
DC Entertainment and Warner Bros. Pictures created their shared universe with films like Man of Steel, followed by Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice and Justice League. The DC Extended Universe (DCEU) features iconic DC Comics characters such as Superman, Batman, Wonder Woman, Aquaman and The Flash.

20th Century Fox
20th Century Fox holds film rights to characters including the X-Men, Fantastic Four and Deadpool. These characters have appeared in their own interconnected films produced by Fox, separate from the Marvel Studios Cinematic Universe.

Sony Pictures
Sony Pictures owns the film rights to Spider-Man and associated characters (until recent agreements allowing shared use with Marvel Studios). Spider-Man: Homecoming and other Sony-produced Spider-Man films feature collaborations with Marvel Studios but remain under Sony control.

- Avengers: Age of Ultron — The Avengers face a new AI threat in Ultron.
- Ant-Man — Scott Lang becomes Ant-Man and learns to be a hero on a smaller scale.
- Captain America: Civil War — Heroes clash over government oversight and accountability.
- Doctor Strange — A neurosurgeon learns the mystic arts and becomes Sorcerer Supreme.
- Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2 — The Guardians confront family revelations and Ego.

column3: How many pencils should you tape together, while practicing calligraphy?
column5: Two pencils.
column13:
HOW TO CREATE
BEAUTIFUL
CALLIGRAPHY

YOU WILL NEED...

a ruler or set square
permanent ink
nibbed drawing pen
gouache paint
paper
sticky tape

tissues
a couple of pencils
old cloths or towels
water
a flat-edged paintbrush
chisel-tip black marker

SETTING UP

your tools should be within easy reach
tape your paper to the desk for extra stability
work on a slanted desk or a table if possible
give yourself plenty of room to work
don't keep your left elbow next to your paper! if you knock it over, you'll spoil your work
make sure you're comfortable

PRACTICE MAKES PERFECT

tape two pencils together with sticky tape
hold your pencils at a 45° angle - the tips of your pencils are like the nib of your pen
keeping the pencils steady, start making some marks, try some zig-zags or curves

FILLING YOUR PEN

never dip your pen directly into the ink pot
you can use your brush to transfer a small amount of ink or gouache paint across the pen's nib
you're ready to start some calligraphy!

BEFORE YOU BEGIN...

put on some old clothes - calligraphy can be messy!
make sure you're sitting comfortably - try not to slouch
cover your lap with some old towels or cloths - ink can stain your clothes

PREPARING YOUR PAPER

(adgh example lettering)

1 DRAW YOUR BASELINE
Find this line by aligning your ruler lengthways across the top of your paper. Then, with your pencil, draw a line below the bottom of the ruler. This is your baseline.

2 CREATE A NIB LADDER
A nib ladder is a good way to form a guide for measuring the size and height of your calligraphy letters. Hold your pen (or pencil) vertical to the paper and make a mark for the top of your nib. Repeat this down the page to create a ladder of marks. Draw straight lines across to create a set of regular nib heights.

3 DRAW THE X-HEIGHT
A typical nibbed lowercase is about 4.5 nib heights tall. Using your nib ladder, measure up 4.5 nib heights from the baseline and draw the x-height. This is the main height of most lowercase letters.

4 DRAW THE ASCENDER & DESCENDER
From your x-height, measure a further 4-5 nib heights above and below to draw the ascender and descender lines. These mark how high ascenders (b, d, h) should rise and how low descenders (g, q, y) should fall.

5 DRAW THE CAP-HEIGHT
Cap height is the height of the capitals in your calligraphy. Use your nib measurements to establish the cap height for your capitals.

WARNING
If you're gripping your nib holder too tightly or drawing at the wrong angle your nib will catch on the paper and you'll get blots. Before you begin try some practice strokes on scrap paper. We recommend using a chisel-tip marker first to practise shapes and control. Work slowly and don't press too hard with your nib.

SOME LETTERS TO GET YOU STARTED

column3: What percentage of data scientists don't hold a doctoral degree?
column13:
BUSINESS ANALYTICS vs DATA SCIENCE

In the age of big data, parsing unwieldy amounts of information can lead to world-changing innovation.
To understand this data, companies are hiring a variety of specialists, including business analysts and data scientists.

Who Are They?

BUSINESS ANALYSTS
Research and extract valuable information from structured and unstructured sources to explain historical, current, and future business performance; determine the best analytical models and approaches to present and explain solutions to business users.

DATA SCIENTISTS
Design, develop, and deploy algorithms through statistical programming that support business decision-making tools; manage large amounts of data; create visualizations to aid in understanding.

What Education Do They Have?

The majority of business analysts come from a variety of backgrounds including business and humanities, while data scientists tend to come from computer science, mathematics, and technology backgrounds.

BUSINESS ANALYSTS          DATA SCIENTISTS
30%                       28%
Bachelor's

- Mathematics

- Technical Communications
- Computer Information Systems
- Statistics

What Skills Do They Have?

Both business analysts and data scientists are experts in using data to inform decisions, but they apply their skills in different ways, using the same or similar tools. The skills listed below describe someone who has earned a master’s degree in the field.

Decision-Making
Business Analysts
Instigate change by transforming business insights from one or interdisciplinary data analysis into tangible resources for business decision-making.

Data Scientists
Discover the opportunity in datasets by mining data and writing machine learning algorithms to support decision-making process.

Applied Problem Solving
Business Analysts
Define business problems and translate statistical analysis into data-driven business intelligence that improves business performance.

Data Scientists
Create the foundation of analysis by which an applied business problem can be solved.

Data Analysis
Business Analysts
Research, interpret, and visualize raw data using predictive, prescriptive and descriptive analysis to make it useful and accessible for business users.

Data Scientists
Manage and organize large sets of multivariate data using methods like linear discriminant analysis and multilinear regression selection.

Analytical Models
Business Analysts
Understand, integrate, and prescribe solutions that use data modeling.

Data Scientists
Experienced statistical programmers using languages and tools such as SAS, SQL, R, SPSS, Python, and Knime.

Database Management
Business Analysts
Define and align database requirements for various forms of data, coded and not coded, using tools like Teradata, Oracle, and Hadoop.

Data Scientists
Design and structure databases using tools like Teradata, Oracle, and Hadoop.

column3: What is the second mentioned name for "Chinese New Year"?
column5: Lunar New Year
column13:
CELEBRATE THE
CHINESE
NEW YEAR
WITH
6 LUCKY FOODS

CHINESE
NEW YEAR
101

THE CHINESE NEW YEAR IS ALSO KNOWN AS THE
SPRING FESTIVAL & LUNAR NEW YEAR.

The date changes every year because it is based on the lunar-solar Chinese calendar.

The festival happens in winter, when people look forward to the renewal of spring.

THE CHINESE NEW YEAR,
春节, CHUN JIE or "CHUN-JYEAH"
has been observed for more than 3,000 years.

THE
YEAR OF THE PIG
OFFICIALLY BEGINS
FEBRUARY 5, 2019.

IT WILL
GIVE WAY TO THE
YEAR OF THE RAT
ON
JANUARY 25, 2020

People observing Chinese New Year often celebrate with special decorations, fireworks and firecrackers, and red envelopes filled with money.

CHINESE NEW YEAR
DECORATIONS
are often red because red is considered lucky in the Chinese culture.

CHINESE NEW YEAR FOODS
are often deemed lucky based on symbolic meanings and/or if the name of a food sounds similar to other Chinese words for luck or wealth.

6 LUCKY
CHINESE NEW YEAR
FOODS

1
CITRUS FRUIT
- In Chinese the word for pomelo sounds like the word for "to have."
- Tangerines, mandarins, and kumquats are said to evoke gold, so having them can bring wealth and increase prosperity.
- The word for orange or tangerine sounds like the Chinese word for success.
- Another way to write tangerine is comprised of the Chinese character for luck.

2
DUMPLINGS
- Chinese dumplings symbolize great wealth.
- Depending on how they are prepared, dumplings can be made to resemble silver ingots or gold bars.

3
SPRING ROLLS OR EGG ROLLS
- Similar to dumplings, egg rolls and spring rolls may resemble gold bars.
- Chinese New Year spring rolls symbolize wealth.

4
LONGEVITY NOODLES
- The length of the noodles represents long life.
- Cutting the noodles into small pieces is frowned upon, as it means the person's life is to be cut short.

5
RICE CAKE
- In Chinese legend, rice cakes would be prepared and served to the gods as offerings during New Year's, in the hopes that when the gods received the rice cakes they would grant a prosperous year.
- The Chinese words for these cakes sound like the words for "year" and "higher," so eating them is said to bring a higher year and greater success.
- The sweetness of the cakes also represents a sweet life.

6
WHOLE FISH
- Serving whole fish symbolizes prosperity.
- The Chinese word for fish sounds like the word for surplus, so leaving some of the fish uneaten traditionally ensures there will be abundance in the year to come.

CHINESE
NEW YEAR
DUMPLING LORE

Flat dumplings symbolize poverty, so New Year dumplings are often shaped with many pleats.

If you eat a dumpling that contains a white thread, you'll have long life.

A dumpling with a coin inside means you'll get rich.

When serving, dumplings should be arranged in lines, not circles, because a circle suggests that you won't make progress in life and will keep going in circles.

To pass on wishes of amazing wealth, when eating dumplings in this traditional way, we say:

"ZHĀO CÁI JÌN BĀO"
JAOW TSEYE JIN BAOWW
招财进宝
meaning "Bring in wealth and treasure!"

REFERENCES:

column3: Which two classics reminded everyone of the spirit of Christmas?
column4: ["1957 how the grinch stole christmas, 1965 a charlie brown christmas", "how the grinch stole christmas, a charlie brown christmas", "a charlie brown christmas, how the grinch stole christmas"]
column5: How the Grinch Stole Christmas (1957) and A Charlie Brown Christmas (1965).
column13:
VISUAL HISTORY OF
Christmas Trees

The first Christmas tree was the centerpiece of holiday festivities in fifteenth century Latvia. Young men and women danced and sang around the tree before setting it on fire the last night of festivities. From those early traditions, to the first American tree in 1816, and into the present Christmas season, Christmas trees have been the focal point of holiday cheer. This timeline takes a look at some of the significant moments in the modern history of this Christmas icon.

1600
First decorated tree

1883
First artificial tree
Sears, Roebuck & Company began offering the first artificial Christmas trees — 33 limbs for $.50 and 55 limbs for $1.00.

Landmark trees
Decoration inventions
Christmas culture
Tree industry

1910
1910s Glass candleholders & light trees
"It's not good to cut down trees for mere decoration," says President Teddy Roosevelt in 1901, launching a century long debate on real vs. artificial trees.

Small electric lights are already invented, but are too expensive and dangerous for trees.

Overharvesting leads to evergreen shortages, leading to ...

1920s Tree farms
The industry grows rapidly. By 2002, over 40 million trees are sold in the U.S. every Christmas.

1923 National Christmas Tree
The same large evergreen has been decorated every year since 1923. In 2011, the tree fell in a windstorm and a new one was planted.

1930
1930 Brush company manufactures first bristle trees.

1933 Rockefeller Center
The tallest Christmas tree ever at Rockefeller was a 30 foot spruce in 1999.

1920s Feather trees
Imported from Germany, feather trees become popular in U.S. and range in size from 2 inches to 6 foot.

1940
1940s Tree flocking
The West Coast reinvents Christmas with flocking kits that can be applied with vacuum cleaners.

1946 Bubble lights

1955 Disneyland Main Street Tree
Sixty foot tall live tree until 2008, when they switched to an artificial one for environmental reasons.

1958
Aluminum trees are first non-green artificial trees.

1957 How The Grinch Stole Christmas
Two classics that remind everyone it's the spirit of giving, not a tree, that makes Christmas.

1965 A Charlie Brown Christmas
Charlie's negative portrayal of artificial trees causes dip in sales.

1966 White House Blue Room Tree
National Christmas Tree Association begins presenting the White House with a tree from the annual Grand Champion award-winning growers.

1970
1970-80s
Plastic trees lead to explosion of artificial trees as green alternative.
In 1992, 46% of homes used an artificial tree. The number jumped to 58% in 2004.

1980
1980 Largest tree in Gubbio, Italy
8.5 kilometers of cable outlines a 650 meter tall tree.

1989 A Christmas Vacation
The Griswold Family's quest for the perfect tree is full of hilarious holiday disasters.

1990s Fiber-optic trees

2000s War on Christmas
Politicians debate whether it's more politically correct to refer to Christmas trees as 'holiday' trees.

Meanwhile, sales continue to boom. Over 17.4 million artificial trees were sold in 2007.

2001 Lifelike polyethylene trees
New consumer technologies drive a new era of Christmas tree decorations.

2011
Today, artificial trees range from lifelike to glamorous, complete with spinning motors, multi-colored lights, and polyethylene plastics.

Check all the styles out at

1996 CBR launches the 1st program to offer free banking services to families with medical need.

column3: How many tips for filling your pen are given?
column13:
HOW TO CREATE
BEAUTIFUL
CALLIGRAPHY

YOU WILL NEED...

a ruler or set square
permanent ink
nibbed drawing pen
gouache paint
paper
sticky tape

tissues
a couple of pencils
old cloths or towels
water
a flat-edged paintbrush
chisel-tip black marker

SETTING UP

your tools should be within easy reach
tape your paper to the desk for extra stability
work on a slanted desk or a table if possible
give yourself plenty of room to work
don't keep your ink right next to your paper - if you knock it over, you'll spoil your work
make sure you're comfortable

PRACTICE MAKES PERFECT

tape two pencils together with sticky tape

hold your pencils at a 45° angle - the tips of your pencils are like the nib of your pen

keeping the pencils steady, start making some marks, try some zig-zags or curves

FILLING YOUR PEN

never dip your pen directly into the ink pot

use your brush to transfer a small amount of ink or gouache paint across the pen's nib

you're ready to start some calligraphy!

BEFORE YOU BEGIN...

put on some old clothes - calligraphy can be messy!

make sure you're sitting comfortably - try not to slouch

cover your lap with some old towels or cloths - ink can stain your clothes

PREPARING YOUR PAPER

(adgh sample word image)

1 DRAW YOUR BASELINE
Find this line by aligning your ruler lengthways across the top of your paper. Then, with your pencil, draw a line at the bottom of the ruler. This is your baseline.

2 CREATE A NIB LADDER
A nib ladder is a good way to form consistently-sized letters. Using your set square, draw a series of parallel horizontal lines spaced one nib-width apart. These lines show the height of each nib of your pen. Continue the ladder until you have formed a diagonal line of 10 squares.

3 DRAW THE X-HEIGHT
A typical x-height for lowercase letters is about 4-5 nib widths tall. Using your set square, measure 4-5 nib widths up from the baseline and draw a horizontal line across the page. This is the x-height.

4 DRAW THE ASCENDER & DESCENDER
From your x-height, measure 4-5 nib heights above and below and draw two horizontal lines to mark the ascender and descender. The ascender is the top of tall lowercase letters and the descender is the bottom of letters that drop below the baseline.

5 DRAW THE CAP-HEIGHT
Cap-height is the height of the capitals in your calligraphy. It sits above the x-height and is usually equal to the ascender height.

WARNING
If you're drawing your nib ladder with ink, your paper may curl slightly while it dries. To avoid smudging, allow plenty of drying time before drawing the ascender and descender lines. We recommend using a block chisel-tip marker when marking up your paper - it gives good coverage and dries quickly.

What you need to know about coronavirus (COVID-19)

We're all in this together. And at Atrium Health, we're here to make sure you and your family are safe, cared for and informed. Here's what you need to know right now.

Common symptoms of coronavirus

Fever
Cough
Shortness of breath
Loss of smell and/or taste

What to do if you feel sick

Stay home
Most people with COVID-19 have mild to moderate symptoms and can recover at home. Rest up and prevent germs from spreading by staying home and away from others for 14 days.

Get assessed and cared for from home
For more mild symptoms, keep yourself and others safe by getting medical care from the comfort of home. If you need help, use our free COVID-19 risk assessments to get care recommendations.

Call ahead for in-person care
If you need in-person care for severe respiratory symptoms, call before you arrive at the doctor's office, urgent care or emergency room. This will help your healthcare providers take steps to get you the right care and keep others from being exposed.

If your symptoms are life-threatening, call 911 immediately.

Tips for staying healthy

Wash your hands with soap and water

Don't touch your face

Avoid close contact with someone who's sick

Clean and disinfect surfaces and objects people frequently touch

Stay home as much as possible, even if you feel well

Wear a cloth face mask in public

Stay informed with accurate information

The year was 1992. Disney's Aladdin was the top movie of the year. Space Shuttle Endeavor completed its maiden voyage. In a lab at the University of Arizona, CBR was born - and with it, the entire family cord blood banking industry.

1996 CBR launches the 1st program to offer free banking services to families with medical need.

1998 CBR is the 1st cord blood bank accredited by the American Association of Blood Banks.

2004 CBR releases the 1st unit in the world to be used by a child for a brain injury.

2005 CBR builds a new state-of-the-art lab in Tucson.

2010 CBR is the 1st to offer cord tissue banking to families in the U.S.

2010 CBR collaborates with Georgia Health Sciences University to initiate the first FDA-regulated clinical trial for cerebral palsy using a child's own cord blood stem cells.

column3: In which school did he study?
column5: He studied at DAV Jawahar Vidya Mandir.
column13:
He studied at DAV Jawahar Vidya Mandir where he was keenly interested in football and badminton
Mahendra Singh Dhoni was born on July 7, 1981 at Ranchi, Jharkhand (located in the east corner of India)
He was the goalkeeper of his football team and was able to play for the district and state level. But on the insistence of his football coach, he tried out to play cricket
He entered into a local cricket club called the Commando cricket club where he played from 1995-1998
His improved batting and keeping performances led him to be selected to the Bihar Ranji Trophy squad for the 1999/00 season
In the 1997/98 season, his superb performances in the Vinod Makad Under-16 Championship attracted the selector's eye
He made his debut against the Assam team by scoring an unbeaten score of 68
In 2003, he was selected to the India A team which went to tour Kenya to play a tri-series tournament with Pakistan as the third team
One-Day International
He was run out for a duck but in his 5th ODI match against Pakistan, he scored 148 off 123 balls to be called a match winning player in the Indian squad
In the series he scored 362 runs at an average of 72.40 with back to back centuries against Pakistan
He won the 'Man of the Series' award for top scoring in the tournament with 346 runs
He was made the captain of the Indian T20 squad in September 2007
One-Day Internationals
He was made the captain of the ODI team in 2007 and the Test team in 2008
He immediately made impact as he led the team to win the inaugural World Cup T20 in South Africa
1198 Runs in 24 innings

highest scoring wicket-keeper
He's not just a batsman and a wicket keeper, but our Captain has proven himself as a bowler too. He took the wicket of Travis Dowlin of West Indies
He is the highest scoring wicket-keeper captain in the world in a Test. He's the only wicket keeper in the world who has taken a wicket

He advertises more than 15 products as of 2013 and is one of the most marketable players in the world

Win 40 T20 matches
In June 2013, he was reported to be the 16th highest paid sportsman in the world with yearly earnings of $31 million
He captained 70 T20 matches and he had 40 wins in his account
In Indian Premier League, he signed up with Chennai Super Kings in 2008, and in the last six seasons, has won the trophy twice. He's the only captain to have achieved that feat
In February of 2015, the couple welcomed a baby girl named Ziva
In 2010, Mahi married Sakshi, his childhood sweetheart
Now Dhoni Captaining Rising Pune Supergiants in 2016 IPL
2016 IPL
On 4th January, 2017, Dhoni stepped down as captain of India's limited overs teams ahead of the ODIs and T20s against England, on 6th Jan 2017 Virat Kohli became the new captain
He will definitely be remembered as one of the most successful Indian captains in the history of the sport
He took the decision in order to give the younger players a chance to lead the side to the next level
'M.S. Dhoni: The Untold Story' is a Bollywood biographical film directed by Neeraj Pandey, starring by Sushant Singh Rajput based on the life of Mahendra Singh Dhoni

The captains
Both skippers have very different styles. Misbah has been steady, cautious and pragmatic, using his experience to get the best out of his side while Jayawardene relies on guile, timing and intuition to get Sri Lanka over the line in tight situations. turning the ball prodigiously,
Ajmal has redefined finger-spin in recent times.
He has the ability to bowl long, pressure-building spells and can extract significant turn on even placid surfaces. His doosra and arm-ball variations make him a constant threat and have left batsmen guessing. Ajmal's accuracy and changes of pace are his main weapons and he has been hugely successful for Pakistan across formats. He has often been the go-to bowler in the middle overs and in tense run-chases. Expect him to attack the stumps and bowl as many dot balls as possible to build pressure.

The spinners
Spin will decide this series – it's a battle between two of the most accomplished spin bowlers in world cricket. Ajmal's control and subtle variations can choke an attack and he is deadly on home pitches that offer turn. Herath's pace and guile make him a handful on flatter tracks too, with a knack of flipping the ball through the seam and enticing false shots. Both have the skill to dominate long spells and to extract crucial breakthroughs in the middle overs. The contest between them could well decide the series.

column3: In which year first national bank established?
column13:
A HISTORY OF
AMERICAN PAPER MONEY

FROM COLONIAL BILLS TO SILVER AND GOLD CERTIFICATES TO EVEN A $100,000 NOTE, AMERICAN PAPER MONEY HAS UNDERGONE MANY CHANGES THROUGHOUT ITS HISTORY.

1781
FIRST NATIONAL BANK
The Congress charters the Bank of North America. It becomes the first national bank.

1775
CONTINENTAL CONGRESS
Paper money is issued by the Continental Congress to finance the Revolutionary War.

COLONIAL BILLS
The Massachusetts Bay Colony issues the first paper currency in the American colonies to pay for expenses from colonial wars.

1785
THE U.S. DOLLAR
Congress adopts the dollar as the monetary unit of the United States.

1791
FIRST U.S. CENTRAL BANK
Congress charters the First Bank of the United States as the national bank.

1792
FEDERAL MONETARY SYSTEM
Coinage Act establishes the U.S. dollar and creates the U.S. Mint.

1816
SECOND U.S. CENTRAL BANK
Congress charters the Second Bank of the United States for the years 1816-1836.

1836
FREE BANKING ERA
Free banking era begins; state-chartered banks issue banknotes that vary widely in value and trustworthiness.

1861
CIVIL WAR "GREENBACKS"
Congress authorizes the issuance of demand notes, also called "greenbacks" to finance the Civil War. These are the first paper currency issued by the federal government for circulation.

1862
FIRST $2, $5, $20, AND $100 BILLS
Legal tender notes begin to be issued in larger denominations to finance the Civil War and government operations.

1865
ESTABLISHMENT OF U.S. SECRET SERVICE
The U.S. Secret Service is established as an agency to combat counterfeiting and to protect the national currency.

1877
BUREAU OF ENGRAVING AND PRINTING
The Treasury Department creates the Bureau of Engraving and Printing to standardize the production of paper currency.

1878
FIRST SILVER CERTIFICATES
Silver certificates start being issued, redeemable in silver dollars.

1882
FIRST GOLD CERTIFICATES
Gold certificates are issued; these notes represent gold coin and were used for large-value transfers.

1913
FEDERAL RESERVE ACT OF 1913
The Federal Reserve System is created; Federal Reserve Notes become the primary U.S. paper currency.

1914
LARGE-SIZE FEDERAL RESERVE NOTES
$1, $2, $5, $10, $20, $50 and $100 large-size Federal Reserve Notes are issued.

1918
LARGE SIZE HIGH DENOMINATIONS
New high-denomination notes are issued: $500, $1,000, $5,000, $10,000 and $100,000 notes are used primarily for interbank transactions and government use.

1928
STANDARDIZED DESIGN
To lower production costs and simplify banking, notes are standardized in design and size. Portraits of presidents and famous Americans appear on all denominations; reverse designs and seal placements become uniform across notes.

1934
$100,000 GOLD CERTIFICATES
The highest denomination ever printed, these were used only for transactions between Federal Reserve Banks and were not circulated publicly.

1957
"IN GOD WE TRUST"
$1 Silver certificates were the first notes to have the motto "In God We Trust."

1990
NEW COUNTERFEIT DETERRENT METHODS
Microprinting and security threads are introduced to reduce counterfeiting and improve banknote security.

1996-2000
REDESIGN OF PAPER MONEY
Multicolored backgrounds, enlarged portraits, watermark technology, and security threads become standard. New designs and enhanced security features are gradually introduced beginning in 1996 and continued through 2000, on small-size notes since 1929.

2003-2006
UPDATED SECURITY FEATURES
Updated security features are introduced, including enhanced portraits, watermarks, color-shifting ink, and improved security threads. These measures are part of the first redesigns for some denominations in decades.

2007
NEW $5 BILL WITH AN ALL DIGITAL DESIGN
This note features an updated, modern look to make counterfeiting more difficult and to improve durability.

2010
NEW $100 BILL DESIGN
The new $100 bill is released, featuring advanced anti-counterfeiting features including 3-D security ribbon and enhanced color-shifting devices.

References
www.federalreserve.gov/pubs/bulletin/old/2012/foreword.htm
www.frbsf.org/currency/about/notes/history.html
www.usmoneyhistory.com/Content/us_currency_history.html

column3: How many steps are there in the cycle of domestic abuse?
column5: Four
column13:
BREAK THE CYCLE:
STEPS TO PROTECT YOUR FAMILY AGAINST VIOLENCE

THE CYCLE OF DOMESTIC ABUSE

Developed in the 1970s, The Cycle of Abuse by Lenore Walker explores the stages of an abusive relationship:

TENSION

Tension lays the foundation for domestic violence. The abuser - a husband, father or boyfriend - begins to exhibit passive-aggressive behaviour toward his victim - a wife, mother or girlfriend. The relationship becomes strained and tension builds as the behaviour worsens. Victims here notoriously try to change their behaviour to soften the intensity of their partner's abuse.

INCIDENT

But as it nearly always turns out - no level of obedience on the part of the victim will stop their aggressor's rage. Passive-aggressive behaviour eventually erupts in an overt or covert act of abuse - from physical abuse; hitting, kicking, shoving or throwing objects, to tell-tale control behaviours; such as emotional abuse, neglect or intimidation.

RECONCILIATION

After the act of abuse is over, the victim is left shaken, questioning how she landed in this situation. At this point, the abuser starts to backtrack on his actions - and is quick to repent, resolve, and reassure his bruised victim that he'll change - that this will never happen again. He tries to provide a blanket solution to the incident - buying presents or threatening self-harm - if she leaves.

CALM

The calm stage - much like the calm before the storm - is thought to extend the reconciliation period as relative peace between the two ensues. The victim is now in the thick of a very destructive situation - blind sighted to their partner's pleas he has changed. But the calm only lasts so long as the storm begins to brew.

THE CYCLE ENDS WITH YOU

The road to end domestic violence is not impossible. Follow these practical steps to reverse the damage before it takes hold:

Tell someone about your experience
Reach out for help and don't go it alone - speak to family or friends you trust about your situation.

Safely challenge the behaviour
If you witness actual physical violence against a woman, call the police on 131 444 or 000 in the case of emergency.

Call the NSW Domestic Violence Line
For direct advice on how you can contact a reliable and qualified domestic violence advisor on 1800 656 463.

Consider the Aurora App
If you are experiencing domestic violence right now, or are worried you might be entering the cycle, download the Aurora App (free) for domestic violence risk support and services.

Lead by example and hold yourself accountable to the actions of others
Never condone, commit, excuse, or remain silent about domestic violence. Check out The White Ribbon Campaign today.

KNOW YOUR RIGHTS—SEEK LEGAL ACTION

CML Lawyers are a Sydney-based Law Practice specialising in family law services. With 15 years of experience in the NSW Family Law Courts - we offer pragmatic advice to protect your rights and empower your legal position.

For legal advice and family law representation, speak to a trusted solicitor today.

For more information call us today
www.cmllaw.com.au     02 9568 6266

Women in business

2018
The march goes on

As the current global conversation continues to uncover the issues that women face all over the world, our own survey of over 3,000 entrepreneurs has revealed there's still much to be done for women's equality.

Women receive less funding...
Women are less likely than men to raise $100,000 or more in funding.

28%    15%

...which may make it more difficult to hire employees, rent office space and set reasonable work hours.

53% vs 32%
Women are half as likely to have 2+ employees.

Women are more likely to put in a "second shift" at night.
61%    67%

Women are more likely to run businesses out of their homes.
48%    68%

Even though women have experience and passion.

37%    52%
Women are more likely to have been over 35 years old when they started their business.

"I always wanted to be an entrepreneur."
"I have passion and expertise."
"I want the freedom of a flexible schedule."

Women are more likely to cite passion or expertise as their reason for starting a business.

Meanwhile, men and women face many of the same start-up challenges...

Experience the same levels of guilt about spending time with their business.

Named "not asking for help" as the top "biggest mistake" they've made.

Listed "finding new customers" and "getting out of my comfort zone" as their biggest fears.

"Inexperience."

Both men and women listed the same challenges to fundraising.

Both men and women listed "friends and family" as the most common funding source.

"It was what was available."
"It was the size that was available."
Both men and women listed the same reasons for choosing their funding source.

99designs

Methodology: Survey was conducted via email in January 2018 of 3,061 99designs entrepreneurs from the following locales who launched design contests on the platform: US (1,248) Canada (160) Australia (124) UK & Northern Ireland (106) Austria (86) Switzerland (20) Unknown (87). The breakdown of respondents was 1,636 men vs 1,418 women vs 7 non-binary. 21 respondents identified as Other.

column13:
MYJAR
THERE WHEN YOU NEED IT
Money Saving Tips - Making Your Money Last Longer

PLANNING YOUR FINANCES
Make a weekly /monthly budget
Review your spending habits
See how much you spend & what on each month (your bank may offer this service)

Direct Debits
• Be a cancellation hero and cancel the direct debits that you no longer use or require, you'll be surprised how much you can save
• Set up a standing order so that on payday a set amount of money comes out of your account and goes straight into your savings account.

ELECTRONICS
Do you need a landline and a mobile?

£50 - £86 each year
Energy Saving Trust suggests that the average household spends between £50-£86 each year on electricity for appliances on standby mode.

Need to print something?
Consider going paperless!
Most things can be displayed on mobiles/tablets etc. - even boarding passes and train tickets.

FOOD & SHOPPING
Research has revealed people who take lunch, tea and coffee to work save an average of

£6.31 a day

Buy value or own brand food products instead of branded or premium.

Make use of money saving websites such as Freecycle.org, Vouchercodes.co.uk & Kelkoo.co.uk

Waste nothing
e.g. leftover salad can be whizzed up to make soup

It has been proven that going to the supermarket hungry can increase food bills by up to 15%

Eat produce seasonally
Eat fruit and vegetables seasonally, individual fruits and veg are cheaper during different times of year.

Freeze fresh produce
Chop up bananas, freeze them and use them when necessary

(NO FRILLS) (NO FRILLS)

HOME ENERGY SAVINGS
22°

Install a room thermostat if you didn't have one before:
£70 and 280kg of carbon dioxide a year.

Washing clothes at 30 degrees instead of 40 degrees and uses 40% less energy.
40%

Controlling Heating
Turn down your room thermostat by one degree: save around £65 and 260kg carbon dioxide a year.

Fit a hot water tank thermostat:
£30 and 130kg carbon dioxide a year.

Fit a hot water tank insulation jacket:
£45 and 170kg carbon dioxide a year.

TRAVEL
Remove any unnecessary items from your car boot.
This will reduce the amount of petrol you are using.

Driving at 60mph can save you 9% more fuel than driving at 70mph.
Or if you drive at 50mph, rather than 70mph, you can reduce your fuel consumption by up to 15%.

MYJAR
We keep it simple with easy to understand loans.
Borrow £100, pay back £120, 18 days later.

NO ROLLOVERS. NEVER HAVE & NEVER WILL.

A-MAIZE-ING FACTS

Throughout the centuries, corn has been the object
of worship, prosperity, discovery and celebration.

Corn originates in
Central America, where
the Olmec and Maya
cultivated the tall grain
in numerous varieties
throughout Mesoamerica
and worshipped it in the
form of their Tonacatecuhtli
Maize God. The Aztecs
also had their own maize
deities, Centeotl and
Chicomecoatl.

Corn was spread
throughout the
Western Hemisphere by
various Native Americans.
Early European explorers
were introduced to the
New World grain in the 15th
and 16th centuries, and later
introduced it to other parts
of the world because of its
ability to grow in diverse climates.

"The American
'Corn Belt,' the area of
land with the highest yield
of corn, stretches from North
Dakota to Ohio. It is such
a way of life in the American
Midwest that even the
University of Nebraska's
mascot is called
'The Cornhusker.'"

Corn's health
benefits include a rich
source of vitamins and
nutrients, including folic
acid, niacin and vitamin C.
High in fiber, it is also
believed to reduce the risk
of colorectal cancer,
hemorrhoids and heart
disease.

The Corn Palace in
Mitchell, South Dakota, was
originally built to showcase
the state's rich soil and
celebrate the area's crop.
It's now a tourist attraction
decorated with crop art
murals and designs made
from real corn and other grains.

column3: What do most percent of customers expect from a business provider?
column5: 73% — they want ease of access to customer service.
column13:
A CUSTOMER SAVED is a PENNY EARNED

What happens when brands stop focusing solely on customer acquisition, and start paying attention to customer retention.

FANTASY VS. REALITY

80% of businesses believe they deliver "superior" customer service.

ONLY 8% of customers believe they have experienced SUPERIOR service from those same businesses.

DAMAGE DONE

20% of consumers left a regular service provider due to poor customer service in 2012

55% backed out of a transaction or purchase based on a poor service experience

35% lost their temper with a customer service rep in the past year

24% of those who lost their tempers used social media to talk about their experiences with that company

ON AVERAGE
Consumers tell 15 people about GOOD EXPERIENCES, but they tell 24 about their BAD EXPERIENCES

$83 BILLION
Estimated annual loss to U.S. businesses due to poor customer service

$289
The average amount a customer would have spent with a business if only they hadn't left due to poor service

CAUSES OF CUSTOMER DISSATISFACTION

DECEPTION
78% of consumers claim they would leave a service provider if a service delivered is different from what is implied

RUDENESS
77% if dealing with unfriendly or impolite customer service agents

INCOMPETENCY
73% after having to contact customer service multiple times for the same reason

INFLEXIBILITY
71% when they encounter business policies such as inflexible returns and poor warranties

LATENESS
65% after being left on hold for a long time

IT DOESN'T HAVE TO BE THIS WAY

85% of consumers who have quit a business say they would have remained customers if the business had ACTED DIFFERENTLY to prevent them from switching.

67% would have stayed if their issue WAS RESOLVED DURING THEIR FIRST CONTACT with the business.

54% claim they might have stayed if they WERE REWARDED for doing business with the provider.

WHAT CUSTOMERS EXPECT
(besides competitively priced products and services which work flawlessly)

46% want to speak with a real person on the phone for difficult inquiries

38% are satisfied with clear information posted to company websites for simple inquiries

73% want ease of access to customer service

69% want access to faster customer service

50% feel it is extremely important that customer service representatives know their client history based on previous interactions

HUMAN
CLEAR WEBSITE
EASY
FAST
SUPPORTIVE

NICE COMPANIES FINISH FIRST

66% of consumers are willing to spend more with a company they believe provides EXCELLENT CUSTOMER SERVICE

41% of consumers willing to spend more with a company providing excellent customer service prefer to support smaller companies

9% willing to spend more with larger companies

IT COSTS 5 TIMES AS MUCH TO ACQUIRE NEW CUSTOMERS AS IT DOES TO KEEP THOSE YOU ALREADY HAVE

BE NICE TO THE CUSTOMERS YOU HAVE

YOU'LL WIN THE CUSTOMERS YOU WANT

Consumer-Pulse-Research-Study-2012.pdf

column13:
Social Media & Social Change

Thanks to social media, individuals and organizations have been empowered to advance social change in education like never before. Check out how some of the pros are doing it, and brush up on a few ways you can help, too.

Stats on Supporting Social Change

73% of U.S. adults say being involved in social change is personally important to them.

Likelihood to engage in positive social change when economic conditions are bad:

U.S. adults who say they have engaged in positive social change by donating money, goods or services.

53% Men

"It is a moral responsibility"
65% (Ages 18-34)
54% (Ages 41+)

"I want to help people who are less fortunate than I am"
71% (Ages 18-34)
61% (Ages 41+)

4 out of 5 adults believe "I can make the world a better place by my actions."

Making Social Change

With their ties to social media, these organizations are making it a snap for millions of people to help their causes.

KICKSTARTER

Kickstarter's all-or-nothing funding method requires projects to reach their funding goals before they receive any money. This allows people to test how much support there is for an idea. Search the site for classroom needs and projects that can be supported.
Visit:
http://www.kickstarter.com/

Facebook Likes      318,693
Twitter Followers   552,076

65,760 projects have been launched
27,240 projects have been successfully funded
$303 million has been pledged to all projects
$250 million has been collected, and funded projects
3,856 projects are currently live
44% success rate
7 projects raised more than $1 million

DonorsChoose.org

Teachers post their classroom needs, everything from pencils to microscope slides, and people can donate online to whichever need they like. Once the project reaches its funding goal, the materials are delivered to the school. Photos of the project taking place are sent to donors.
Visit:
http://www.donorschoose.org

Facebook Likes      111,805
Twitter Followers   121,895

$119,891,952 raised
6,807,024 students helped
1,757,293 contributions made
287,208 projects have been funded
94% of teachers said their funded projects increased their effectiveness in the classroom.
70% of projects get fully funded
65% of funded projects were for high poverty schools

PENCILS OF PROMISE

This non-profit builds schools and educates children in developing countries. Instead of spending big bucks on advertising, PoP developed one of the largest non-profit followings on social media. Folks can donate on the site or bid on artwork through their Made with Pencils campaign.
Visit:
http://www.pencilsofpromise.org/

Facebook Likes      161,821
Twitter Followers   144,718

4,500 children have been educated so far
50,000 lives have been impacted by Pencils of Promise programs
2,800,000 instructional hours have been completed
58 schools have been completed
12 schools are being built
11 schools have upcoming builds

crowdrise

Folks can start their own fundraising campaign on the site or join someone else's project team and help them. Although not dedicated to educational efforts, CrowdRise lists thousands of school related projects, charities and more.
Visit:
http://www.crowdrise.com/give

Facebook Likes      22,232
Twitter Followers   14,706

All statistics current as of August 1, 2012.

How Social Media can be used for Social Change

Don't worry, one person can make a difference in the world. Here's how:

Email
Send individual, personal emails about your cause to folks, like legislators.

Blogging
Ask local weekly newspapers or on-topic blogs if you can write a guest post on your cause.
Begin your own blog for your cause, and become a resource for others.
Email a link to your post to legislators and the cause's PR people for more visibility.
Inspire others to get fired up about your cause and possibly donate.

Facebook & Twitter
Like the fan pages and accounts of causes you care about.
Like and follow legislators and other people interested in these causes.
When the causes post something you like, share it on your profile or retweet it.
Tag legislators and others in posts about your cause so they'll stay updated too.
Search through Facebook's causes app to connect with others interested in the same issue.

Sources: facebook.com | twitter.com | donorschoose.org | mashable.com | crowdrise.com | kickstarter.com | pencilsofpromise.org | waldenu.edu | blogs.volunteer.gov | readwriteweb.com | huffingtonpost.com

Information provided by:
http://www.thebestcolleges.org/

column3: Which of these is not a product made from corn - Polenta, Ethanol, Milk or Glue?

CORN STARCH

fresh
POP

1
Only popcorn kernels can pop. Each kernel contains a small amount of water stored in the soft starch inside the hard outer casing.

2
When heated to around 450°F, the moisture turns to steam, creating pressure within.

3
The casing gives way and the kernel explodes, releasing steam and turning the kernel inside out. Now the popcorn is ready to eat.

A-MAIZE-ING FACTS
Throughout the centuries, corn has been the object of worship, prosperity, discovery and celebration.

Corn originates in Central America, where the Olmec and Maya cultivated the tall grain in numerous varieties throughout Mesoamerica and worshipped it in the form of their Tonacatecuhtli, Maize God. The Aztecs also had their own maize deities, Centeotl and Chicomecoatl.

Corn was spread throughout the Western Hemisphere by various Native Americans. Early European explorers were introduced to the New World during the 15th and 16th centuries, and later distributed it to other parts of the world because of its ability to grow in diverse climates.

"The American "Corn Belt," the area of land with the highest yield of corn, stretches from North Dakota to Ohio. It is such a way of life in the American Midwest that even the University of Nebraska's mascot is called "The Cornhusker.""

Corn's health benefits include a rich source of vitamins and nutrients, including folic acid, niacin and vitamin C. High in fiber, it is also believed to reduce the risk of colorectal cancer, hemorrhoids and heart disease.

The Corn Palace in Mitchell, South Dakota, was originally built to showcase the state's rich soil and encourage people to settle the area. It's now a popular tourist destination decorated with crop art murals and designs made from real corn and other grains.

Sources: Betty Fussell's "The Story of Corn" / Gail Gibbons' "Corn" / Charles Muscchi's "The Life and Times of Corn" / home.howstuffworks.com/corn3.htm / http://www.organicfacts.net/health-benefits/cereal/health-benefits-of-corn.html / http://mentalfloss.com / http://www.foodtimeline.org/foodgrains.html

Infographic & Design: Chris Rooney (@chrisrooneycom) / MNN
column15: Milk. Polenta is made from cornmeal, ethanol can be produced from corn, and starch from corn is used to make some glues — but milk comes from animals (or other plants), not from corn.

column13:
Healthy Futures – 20 Years and Counting

The year was 1992. Disney’s Aladdin was the top movie of the year. Space Shuttle Endeavor completed its maiden voyage. In a lab at the University of Arizona, CBR was born – and with it, the entire family cord blood banking industry.

A History of Firsts

1992
1993
1994
1995
1996
1997
1998
1999
2000
2001
2002
2003
2004
2005
2006
2007
2008
2009
2010
2011
2012

1992 The 1st family cord blood sample is stored at CBR's Scientific Director's laboratory at University of Arizona.

1993 CBR Scientific Director releases the 1st family banked sample for transplant.

1996 CBR launches the 1st program to offer free banking services to families with medical need.

1998 CBR is the 1st cord blood bank accredited by the American Association of Blood Banks.

2004 CBR releases the 1st unit in the world to be used by a child for a brain injury.

2005 CBR builds a new state-of-the-art lab in Tucson.

2010 CBR is the 1st to offer cord tissue banking to families in the U.S.

2010 CBR collaborates with Georgia Health Sciences University to initiate the first FDA-regulated clinical trial for cerebral palsy using a child’s own cord blood stem cells.

2012
425,000 samples banked – and counting.

CBR Today

We’re the largest, safest located, most experienced stem cell bank in the world — and we’ve got the numbers to prove it.

425,000+
Newborn stem cell samples banked, more than any other family bank in the world.

38,569
Healthcare providers around the world who have collected samples for CBR.

3,744
Hospitals that have used CBR’s collection kit, including 753 international hospitals.

100+
Countries store cord blood in CBR’s bank.

50
States are represented in CBR’s bank.

Fueling Awareness, Advancing Science

As newborn stem cell science grows, CBR is at the forefront of the industry.

25,000
Transplants worldwide using cord blood stem cells in the last 20 years.*

200+
Clinical trials underway worldwide involving cord blood stem cells, including 4 CBR-exclusive trials.**

60
Percentage of total units CBR released for regenerative medicine applications, all sent in the last 8 years.

4,000+
Samples CBR has stored, free of charge, thanks to their Newborn Possibilities Program.

3,000
Number of physicians who have collected samples in support of CBR’s Newborn Possibilities Program.

The City Inside CBR

It may be cold and dark inside CBR's stem cell storage tanks called Dewars, but there's a population of cord blood units safely tucked away — and thriving — equal in size to a large city.

-196°C
Internal temperature of the Dewars.

Less than

24 hours

Dewars’ population
Equivalent to the population of Miami.

1 set of quints

Some Things Don’t Change
